title: NÒMARHYTHM TEXTILE and JOURNAL STANDARD Launch Exclusive Mesh Jacket in Signature Print
summary:
Summary

Independent Japanese label NÒMARHYTHM TEXTILE has partnered with retail imprint JOURNAL STANDARD for an exclusive apparel releaseThe collaboration showcases the brand's unique design approach by embedding an original hand-drawn bandanna print onto a lightweight mesh baseThe specialized outerwear piece is currently available for purchase through official domestic online storefronts

NÒMARHYTHM TEXTILE and JOURNAL STANDARD have officially come together to launch the exclusive NÒMARHYTHM TEXTILE for JS Mesh Jacket. Marking a major collaborative milestone for the label, the summer-ready layer delivers a highly artistic aesthetic explicitly tailored for the contemporary streetwear landscape.The lightweight jacket highlights the brand's historic commitment to free-flowing, hand-drawn textile designs that operate outside traditional seasonal trends. For this exclusive release, the design utilizes an original, intricately detailed bandanna motif applied directly onto an airy mesh fabric base. The conceptual layout is engineered to look less like a rigid printed garment and more like an organic extension of the wearer, offering a breezy sensation optimal for warm-weather climates.Constructed to provide both high-grade ventilation and a distinct visual texture, the relaxed-fitting outerwear piece features clean structural lining alongside casual proportions. The garment effortlessly balances functional everyday comfort with sophisticated lifestyle styling. By blending heritage pattern-making techniques with modern mesh architecture, the joint project effectively captures the poetic yet experimental design philosophy that defines the newly evolved imprint.The NÒMARHYTHM TEXTILE for JS exclusive Mesh Jacket is available now via online.

Click here to view full gallery at Hypebeast
